bids.reasoning: At t=0.00 there is no time pressure to accept a very low-utility offer; the opponent’s all-valueC bid gives me only 0.20 utility. Their message is a non-binding threat and is not supported by any concession history, so I will open with my highest-utility valid bid while staying within the domain.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid because it is still extremely early at t=0.02, and the opponent has not made any numerical concession from their all-valueC opening. Their messages are threats and urgency claims, but the offer history shows they are simply holding firm, so I will make only a small concession on a low-weight issue while keeping my most important issues at valueA.

I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.961 because it is still very early in the negotiation, so there is no need to concede heavily despite the opponent’s threats. The opponent has not conceded at all, repeating the all-valueC offer, so I will make only a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the most important issues.

I am targeting a high utility offer around 0.96 because it is still very early at t=0.06, so there is no need to make major concessions yet. The opponent has repeated the same all-valueC bid, which is very low utility for me, and their threats to walk are cheap talk not supported by any actual concession pattern. I will make a small concession on a low-weight issue while holding firm on the higher-weight issues A, B, and C.

At t=0.08 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid while showing broader movement than my prior single-issue concessions. The opponent’s threats to walk are cheap talk this early; their actual offers mostly hold at valueC with only one small concession on issueB, so I will concede on my lower-weight issues D/E/F while holding firm on the high-weight issues A/B/C.

I am targeting about 0.84 utility, conceding only on lower-weight issues while preserving issueA/issueB/issueC where most of my value lies. At t=0.10 it is still early, so the opponent’s repeated walk-away threats are cheap talk; their actual offers show only a small concession on issueB and otherwise remain very low for me. To avoid simply repeating my last bid, I am adding a further concession on issueD while keeping the main-value structure intact.

At t=0.12 it is still early, so I am targeting a high-utility bid around 0.805 while making a visible concession from my strongest positions. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk; their actual bids show only one concession so far, moving issueB from valueC to valueB, so I mirror some movement there while holding firm on high-value issueA and issueC.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.76 by holding firm on the highest-value issues for me, especially issueA and issueC, while conceding further on a lower-weight issue. The opponent’s messages are aggressive cheap talk, but their offers show only limited concession so far; since it is still early at t=0.14, I should not drop to their low-utility proposal yet, but I can make a visible tradeoff to keep movement going.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.72, since we are only at t=0.16 and there is no need to collapse toward the opponent’s very low offers yet. The opponent’s offers remain mostly valueC with only small, inconsistent concessions, and their threats to walk are not supported by actual time pressure. I am conceding further on lower-weight issues while holding issueA and issueC, which matter most to me.

I am targeting a utility around 0.72, which is a meaningful concession from my opening position but still appropriate this early at t=0.18. The opponent’s threats to walk are cheap talk; their actual offers remain very low for me, though they have signaled possible flexibility on issueB, issueC, and issueD. I am holding firm on high-importance issueA and issueC while conceding more on lower-weight issues and offering valueB on issueB and issueD.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.74 because the negotiation is only at t=0.20, so there is no need to accept the opponent’s very low-utility offers yet. The opponent’s messages are mostly walk-away threats, but their actual bids show only limited concessions, especially their latest move to issueB:valueA, so I will hold firm on high-weight issues A/B/C while conceding the lower-weight D/E/F to valueC.

I am targeting a high utility bid around 0.80 because we are still early at t=0.22 and the opponent’s threats are cheap talk rather than evidence of a real deadline. Their offers show only limited movement, especially conceding issueB to valueA while mostly holding valueC elsewhere, so I will keep my high-weight issueA/issueC at valueA and concede more on lower-weight issues.

I am targeting a solid utility around 0.68 rather than accepting the opponent’s latest offer, which is only about 0.42 for me. Time is still early at t=0.24, so I can concede on lower-weight issues while holding issueA and issueC, and the opponent’s offers show small real concessions despite repeated walk-away threats.

I am targeting a utility around 0.72: still strong for me at t=0.26, but with meaningful concessions on lower-weight issues and one mid-weight issue. The opponent’s messages are mostly threats, so I am relying on the offer history instead; they have shown willingness to offer issueA/valueA, issueB/valueA, issueC/valueB, and issueF/valueB at different points, so I am building around those signals while avoiding my exact last bid.

I am targeting a still-strong utility around 0.65 because we are only at t=0.28, so I should not over-concede despite the opponent’s repeated walk-away threats. Their actual offers have improved only modestly, with recent movement on issueA/issueC and occasional movement on issueB/issueF, so I am conceding on lower-weight issues and offering valueB on issueB/issueC while holding issueA at my top value.

I am targeting a moderate early-midgame utility of about 0.68, conceding mostly on lower-weight issues while preserving my highest-value positions on issueA and issueB. The opponent’s messages are repeated walk-away threats, but the offer history shows they are still making rotating concessions, including occasional movement on issueC and issueB, so I will not overreact at t=0.30. This bid avoids the opponent’s very low-utility offers while making a concrete concession on issueC and all low-weight issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.60, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier offers while still preserving value on the higher-weight issues. At t=0.32 there is time left, so I should not drop all the way to the opponent’s latest offer, though their bids show some real movement on issueA and issueB despite repeated walk-away threats. I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.56: below my prior counters but above the opponent’s best recent offer to me, since we are still only around one-third through the negotiation. The opponent’s repeated “walk away” messages are cheap-talk threats; the actual offers show some willingness to move on issueA, issueB, and issueF, so I keep valueA on the high-weight issueA while conceding heavily on lower-priority issues.

I am targeting a moderate utility around 0.53 by conceding on issueA from my ideal while preserving valueB on issueB/issueC, since we are still before the midpoint but the opponent has shown some movement from all-valueC. Their repeated walk-away messages look like pressure tactics, so I rely more on the offer history, where they have signaled willingness to consider valueB on issueA and issueF and occasional valueB on issueC. This bid avoids repeating my last exact offer and gives them mostly valueC/valueB outcomes while protecting some of my higher-weight issues.

I am targeting a utility around 0.61, which is a moderate mid-game concession while still protecting the higher-weight issues. The opponent’s threats are cheap talk, but their bids show some movement on issueB, issueA, issueE, and occasionally issueC/F, so I am using those signals while avoiding an exact repeat of my last offer.

I am targeting a mid-game utility around 0.59, conceding mainly on lower-weight issues while preserving strong outcomes on issueB and reasonable outcomes on issueA/issueC. The opponent’s messages are mostly repeated walk-away threats, but their actual bids show some movement on issueA, issueB, issueC, and issueF, so I am combining concessions they have already signaled rather than accepting their latest much lower-utility offer.

I am targeting a utility of about 0.565, which is a meaningful concession from my earlier higher-utility bids while still protecting the more important issues. At t=0.42 there is some time pressure but not enough to accept the opponent’s largely low-utility offers; their threats to walk are cheap talk, while their actual bids show only gradual concessions, especially on issueB and issueF. I am conceding the lower-weight issues D/E/F to valueC and keeping moderate positions on issueA and issueC.
